text.title: DORA + Reliability Metrics Report Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the dora-metrics methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Deploy events MUST be ingested in a CDEvents-compatible schema (or equivalent: timestamp, service, env, commit SHA, success bool); ad-hoc CSV is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a typed event schema, lead-time/MTTR computation drifts and cannot be reconciled across teams.

rule.statement: Lead Time for Changes MUST be `prod_deploy_timestamp - commit_timestamp` for the latest commit in the deployed SHA; merge-time approximations are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Merge-time hides queueing in CI/CD; commit-to-prod is the true user-felt lead time.

rule.statement: Deployment Frequency MUST be reported as a rolling 7-day count per service; monthly aggregates hide regressions.
rule.rationale: Long aggregation windows mask the very degradation the metric is meant to surface.

rule.statement: Change Failure Rate MUST be computed as `incidents_within_24h / deploys` where incidents reference a deploy commit; opinion polls are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Self-reported CFR is biased low; deploy-linked incident data is the only objective source.

rule.statement: MTTR MUST include detection-to-mitigation, not just response-to-mitigation; clock starts when the incident's first symptom is observable.
rule.rationale: Hiding detection time turns MTTR into a vanity metric that improves while user-visible outage time stays flat.

rule.statement: Report MUST include Reliability (SLO attainment %) alongside the four DORA metrics; the 2024 update positions Reliability as the fifth pillar.
rule.rationale: Pure throughput metrics encourage shipping at the expense of stability; Reliability is the counterbalance.

rule.statement: If the team has no production deploys yet (pre-product), this methodology MUST be skipped.
rule.rationale: Computing DORA on zero deploys yields noise.
